title: Optimistic locking on record edits — If-Match via updated_at (GLOBAL-CONC-001 / DB-005)
business_reason: Two admins editing the same record could silently overwrite each other. A kernel-level optimistic lock rejects an edit whose If-Match token (the updated_at last read) no longer matches.

design.kernel: updateModuleRecord extracts an optional expected_updated_at (If-Match token) from the PATCH payload; if present and the row's current updated_at differs, it throws ConflictError -> 409. Opt-in (no token = no check), no schema change (reuses updated_at as the version token).
design.mapping: the makeStaffItemHandlers PATCH (and POST) handlers now map ConflictError -> 409 with the specific message.

param_14.question: What stops two admins overwriting each other's edits?
param_14.answer: An optimistic lock: a client sends the updated_at it last read as an If-Match token (expected_updated_at). If the record changed since, the kernel rejects the edit with 409 Conflict and the stale write is not applied — the second admin is told to reload. It is opt-in and needs no schema change.
param_14.security_implications: prevents silent lost updates; conflict surfaced explicitly; opt-in so existing callers are unaffected.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: have edit UIs send the token; extend to transitions if needed.
